```json
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-service",
  "component_conditions": ["failed"],
  "percent_complete": 100,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Auxiliary heat is locked on continuously while the unit is in service.",
    "Continuous auxiliary heat operation indicates a control sequence or thermostat fault rather than a normal heating call.",
    "Likely causes include a failed heat sequencer/staging relay, shorted contactor coil, failed thermostat heat relay, or incorrect wiring/firmware in the control sequence.",
    "Continuous aux heat wastes energy, causes overheating, and can damage downstream equipment."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the auxiliary heat circuit immediately to prevent equipment damage and energy waste.",
    "Inspect thermostat configuration and wiring for stuck heat outputs or incorrect staging setpoints.",
    "Test the heat sequencer, contactor coils, and relays for welded contacts or failed coils.",
    "Verify control sequence logic against the manufacturer's manual and the approved submittal sequence of operation.",
    "Replace failed components and re-test all heating stages under normal and emergency conditions before returning to service."
  ],
  "rationale": "Auxiliary heat locked on in a commercial HVAC-R system is an abnormal operating condition. In normal operation, auxiliary heat stages are modulated or staged by the thermostat/control system based on load and outdoor temperature. A locked-on condition points to a failed control component (e.g., welded sequencer contacts, stuck relay, or thermostat output) or a sequence-of-operation fault. Because the system is in service, this represents a failed component condition and a high-risk energy/safety issue, warranting a fail decision until corrected.",
  "references": [
    "mfr manuals",
    "ASHRAE 90.1 - heating control requirements",
    "NFPA 70 (NEC) - electrical controls and disconnecting means"
  ]
}
```
